faq.sectionHeadline: Questions people ask before booking the Starter

items.q: How long does it really take, from kickoff to live?
items.a: 3 to 5 working days once I have your copy, images and brand basics in hand. I block your slot in the calendar and commit to the date — no ghosting, no surprise extensions.

items.q: What do I need to provide before we start?
items.a: Your logo in vector (SVG or PDF), the texts for each section, 5–10 good photos, and a clear goal for the page (booking, inquiry, newsletter…). I send a simple checklist the same day you book.

items.q: Do I own the website after launch?
items.a: Yes — 100%. The code lives in your GitHub repo, the domain stays on your account, and the CMS credentials are yours. If we ever stop working together, you keep everything.

items.q: Can I edit text and images myself later?
items.a: Yes. I set up a lightweight headless CMS (Decap by default) so you can update copy, images and metadata from a normal login, no code required. A 30-minute training call is included.

items.q: What if I need more than one page?
items.a: Starter is built around a single long-form page with up to 4 sections. If you need 2–6 pages, Growth is the right fit. More than that — we talk about Scale.

items.q: Do you build on WordPress, Webflow or Squarespace?
items.a: By default I use Astro + a headless CMS for speed and long-term stability. I can build on WordPress, Webflow or Framer on request, but I'll be transparent about the performance trade-offs first.

items.q: How many revisions are included?
items.a: Two revision rounds on the live staging URL are built into every Starter project. Further changes after launch go through the Care retainer or a small hourly rate.

items.q: What happens if something breaks after launch?
items.a: You get 2 weeks of free post-launch bug cover. After that, a €190/month Care plan keeps the site monitored, updated and patched — or you can call me ad-hoc.

founder.name: Francisco Cortez
founder.message: No agency layers, no juniors, no handoffs — you get the person who'll actually build it. From the first call to launch, and the years after.

faq.sectionHeadline: Questions before architecting your Scale platform

items.q: Which CMS do you recommend — Sanity, Storyblok or Decap?
items.a: For editorial teams and rich schemas I default to Sanity. For non-technical editors who need a visual UI, Storyblok. For solo founders or small teams already on GitHub, Decap. I'll recommend the right one after a 30-minute briefing.

items.q: Can my team edit content without breaking the design?
items.a: Yes. I model the schema around block components with strict validation, so editors can't accidentally change layouts or push malformed data. Every change is a version-controlled draft until it's published.

items.q: Can I add more languages later?
items.a: Absolutely. The build is i18n-ready from day one. Adding a new locale (fr, it, pt…) is a scoped project, not a rebuild — typically 1–2 weeks depending on translation volume.

items.q: How do you handle advanced SEO for multi-page sites?
items.a: Every route ships with schema.org JSON-LD (Organization, Service, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage, Article where relevant), hreflang links for each language, and automated XML sitemaps. I also configure Search Console and a basic GEO plan for AI search.

items.q: Will I need a developer on retainer to keep it running?
items.a: Not for content — that's the whole point of the headless CMS. For platform-level work (new features, integrations, performance tuning) most Scale clients move to a Care retainer or buy hourly blocks as needed.

items.q: Do you migrate content from my current site?
items.a: Yes. I script migrations from WordPress, Webflow, Contentful, Notion or custom sources. Migration is quoted separately after a quick content audit — pricing scales with the volume and structure complexity.

items.q: How are animations and ViewTransitions handled on dynamic routes?
items.a: Animations are component-level (GSAP or native View Transitions API), so they stay intact regardless of how many content entries you publish. I explicitly test across CMS content to avoid layout shift on long-tail pages.

items.q: What's the total cost of ownership in year one?
items.a: Build from €3,800. CMS hosting (Sanity / Storyblok free tiers or ~€15–50/mo). Edge hosting (Vercel / Netlify — often free). Optional Care retainer from €190/mo. Most Scale clients land between €5k and €7k all-in for year one.

founder.name: Francisco Cortez
founder.message: No agency layers, no juniors, no handoffs — you get the person who'll actually build it. From the first call to launch, and the years after.

faq.sectionHeadline: Questions before committing Growth to your funnel

items.q: Will this page actually lift my ROAS?
items.a: A faster, CRO-informed landing page reliably reduces cost-per-lead for paid campaigns — every extra second of load time drops conversion materially. I don't promise numbers I can't own, but the setup removes the friction that's usually dragging your ROAS down.

items.q: Do you integrate with Meta, Google Ads and my CRM?
items.a: Yes. Meta Pixel, Google Ads conversion tags, GA4, GTM, and CRM webhooks (HubSpot, Brevo, Mailchimp, Notion, Zapier) are part of the base build. Just tell me what stack you run.

items.q: Can I A/B test headlines and sections?
items.a: The page is built in modular sections — headline, hero, social proof, offer, form — so you can swap or split-test any of them without a full rebuild. I can also wire up a testing tool (VWO, Vercel Analytics, Statsig) if you have one.

items.q: How fast does the page load?
items.a: Target is ≥ 95 on Lighthouse and green Core Web Vitals on 3G. In practice that's usually a sub-1.5 s Largest Contentful Paint — fast enough that your paid click doesn't die waiting.

items.q: Can I run multiple variants for different campaigns?
items.a: Yes. I can clone the page as a route (e.g. /berlin, /autumn-offer) with small copy/creative tweaks — either manually or as dynamic routes pulling from a CMS.

items.q: Is tracking and Consent Mode v2 included?
items.a: Yes. Google Consent Mode v2, cookie banner, GDPR-compliant analytics defaults (Plausible or cookieless GA4), and proper event tagging all come in the Growth package.

items.q: What if my offer or conversion goal changes later?
items.a: The form, CTA and headline are all CMS-editable. Bigger changes (new sections, new tracking, new integrations) go through a Care retainer or quick hourly block.

items.q: Do you also manage ad creative or just the landing page?
items.a: I focus on the landing page, tracking, and conversion infrastructure. Ad creative and media buying stay with you or your ads partner — I'm happy to collaborate directly with them.

founder.name: Francisco Cortez
founder.message: No agency layers, no juniors, no handoffs — you get the person who'll actually build it. From the first call to launch, and the years after.

faq.sectionHeadline: Questions before starting your Care retainer

items.q: What exactly do you do every month?
items.a: Dependency and security updates, Core Web Vitals monitoring, Lighthouse check, uptime monitoring, a monthly report, and a block of implementation hours for small changes (copy, images, new section, tracking tweaks).

items.q: Can I cancel at any time?
items.a: Yes — Care is month-to-month, no lock-in. Cancel with 14 days notice and you keep every commit, repo and asset. Nothing is held hostage.

items.q: What's the difference between Care and regular web hosting?
items.a: Hosting just serves files. Care keeps the site fast, secure and current: patches vulnerabilities, monitors performance, fixes regressions, and gives you a direct line to a developer who already knows your codebase.

items.q: Do you cover security patches and CVE alerts?
items.a: Yes. Automated dependency updates, CVE alerts via GitHub, and manual patching for anything that needs a human. Critical vulnerabilities get same-day turnaround.

items.q: How fast do you respond if the site goes down?
items.a: Mon–Fri, 9–18 CET: first reply inside 4 hours. Uptime monitoring pings me automatically before most clients notice. Emergency / out-of-hours cover is available as an optional add-on.

items.q: Do I get priority over new-build clients?
items.a: Yes. Care clients jump the queue for quick fixes and small tasks. New builds get scheduled around existing Care load, not the other way around.

items.q: Do unused implementation hours roll over?
items.a: Included hours roll over one month. Beyond that they expire — it keeps the retainer honest for both of us and avoids hoarding. Extra hours are always available at a reduced rate.

items.q: What happens if I want to redesign later?
items.a: Care is separate from build work. When you're ready for a redesign we scope it like a new project (Starter / Growth / Scale), and the retainer continues to cover the current site until the new one ships.

founder.name: Francisco Cortez
founder.message: I started in design before moving to code, so I know what it feels like when a dev ruins a Figma file. That won't happen here — your file is the spec, full stop.

faq.sectionHeadline: Questions designers ask before starting a partnership

items.q: Do you really stay white-label — will my client ever learn your name?
items.a: No. I never appear in client communications, invoices, or credits unless you explicitly introduce me. Repos, deploy accounts and CMS access all carry your studio's name.

items.q: How do we handle the NDA?
items.a: I sign your NDA (or a standard mutual one) before I open the Figma file. No exceptions. Nothing is shared, sub-contracted or put in a public portfolio without written permission.

items.q: What happens if we part ways mid-project?
items.a: Repo ownership is yours from the first commit, so you keep a working, documented build no matter what. I bill only up to the last delivered milestone — no exit fees, no hostage situations.

items.q: What stacks do you build in?
items.a: My defaults are Astro and Next.js with Tailwind. I also build in SvelteKit, vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, and plug into headless CMS (Sanity, Storyblok, Decap, Prismic). I'll flag honestly if your target stack is outside my comfort zone.

items.q: Do you handle Figma to Webflow or Framer too?
items.a: Yes, on request. My sweet spot is code-first builds — but I've shipped in Webflow and Framer for designers who need a no-code handover. Pricing and timelines differ; I'll share both paths after the brief.

items.q: How do you charge — hourly or per-project?
items.a: Your call. Per-project fixed fee for scoped Figma handoffs, weekly rate for longer engagements, or €80/hour for ad-hoc work. Invoices are sent through my studio; your client never sees them.

items.q: Can you meet a tight 1-week sprint?
items.a: Yes, with a +35% urgency rate. I keep capacity for emergencies but need at least 48 hours of heads-up so I can reshuffle cleanly without dropping another client's timeline.

items.q: Do you handle CMS training for my clients?
items.a: Yes. I record a Loom walkthrough of the CMS in your studio's branding, and optionally run a live 45-minute training with your client (€250 add-on). You decide whether I'm present or you forward it yourself.

designerBanner.text: Let's turn your Figma designs into lightning-fast, pixel-perfect code.

hero.headline: I future-proof brands for the AI search era with high-performance digital architectures.

items.title: The Strategy
items.description: Search has evolved. I map out your visibility not just for Google, but for AI engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity. I focus on entity optimization to ensure your brand becomes a cited authority.

items.title: The Execution
items.description: I build lightning-fast, mobile-first platforms. Every pixel is optimized for conversions, and every line of code is structured with Schema markup so AI crawlers can easily extract and cite your data.

items.title: The Growth
items.description: You get a digital asset that ranks higher in traditional search, gets recommended by AI assistants, and turns that high-quality, targeted traffic into loyal clients.

values.title: I engineer for AI retrieval
values.description: If an AI crawler cannot read your site, you don't exist in the new web. I deliver clean architecture, structured data, and ultra-fast load times so you are always the primary source.

values.title: I design conversion funnels
values.description: As AI answers more questions directly, the traffic that does reach your site is highly qualified. I build frictionless user experiences that guide these high-intent visitors straight to checkout or contact.

values.title: I build resilient partnerships
values.description: The digital landscape changes daily. Whether adapting to new LLM algorithms, optimizing for AI Overviews, or launching a new campaign, I act as your dedicated technical growth partner for the long haul.

success.escapeRoute: Not ready to book a time yet? No problem. We've sent a link to your inbox. In the meantime, explore our

founderVideo.title: 30 seconds from your founder
founderVideo.caption: A quick hello so you know who's picking up the call.

scale.title: 3 things to bring to the call

items.label: One site you admire, one you don't
items.detail: URLs are enough. I'll use them to calibrate taste + anti-patterns.

items.label: Your two top-ranking competitors
items.detail: The ones you actually want to outrank, not the obvious giants.

items.label: One metric you want to move in 90 days
items.detail: Qualified leads, demo bookings, organic traffic — pick one.

partnership.title: 3 things to bring to the call

items.label: A sample Figma file (view access is fine)
items.detail: Any recent project shows me your grid, components and handoff style.

items.label: One upcoming project you'd want to ship together
items.detail: Even rough scope helps me estimate realistic cadence + pricing.

items.label: Your ideal handoff rhythm
items.detail: Weekly sync? Async Loom? I adapt — knowing upfront saves a round.

default.title: 3 things to bring to the call

items.label: A working domain name (or one you're about to buy)
items.detail: If you don't have one yet, bring 2–3 options we can sanity-check.

items.label: Your logo + 4–8 photos in one folder
items.detail: Drive, Dropbox, iCloud — anywhere you can share a link from.

items.label: One sentence describing your ideal client
items.detail: Who you want walking through your door, not who already does.

projects.title: Carma Retreats
projects.description: A sanctuary for yoga and mindfulness in Sri Lanka. I designed a digital experience that reflects their core values: Connection, Culture, and Community.

projects.title: Cornelia Jaeger
projects.description: Holistic Occupational Therapy and Coaching for children and parents. A professional platform focused on developmental support and family well-being.

sections.heading: 1. Privacy at a Glance
sections.body: The following information provides a simple overview of what happens to your personal data when you visit this website. Personal data is any data that can be used to personally identify you. Data processing on this website is carried out by the website operator.

sections.heading: 2. Hosting and Infrastructure
sections.body:
Vercel: Our website is hosted by Vercel (Vercel Inc., USA). Data is processed based on Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR to ensure secure and efficient provision of our services. We have concluded a data processing agreement (DPA) with the provider.

Cloudflare: To secure and optimize loading speeds, we use the Content Delivery Network (CDN) provided by Cloudflare Inc. This serves our legitimate interest in a stable and secure website performance according to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.

sections.heading: 3. Controller (Responsible Party)
sections.body:
The party responsible for data processing on this website is:

Jose Francisco Cortez Moreno
Klarastr. 8
12459 Berlin
Germany

  • Email: legal@base-pages.com
  • Phone: +491756975657

sections.heading: 4. Your Rights (GDPR)
sections.body:
As a user, you have the following rights at any time:

  • Right to information about your stored data.
  • Right to demand the correction or deletion of your data.
  • Right to revoke any previously given consent for data processing.
  • Right to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority.
  • Right to data portability in a standard format.

sections.heading: 5. Analysis and Marketing Tools
sections.body:
We use analysis and tracking tools only after receiving your express consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR):

  • Google Analytics & Tag Manager: Used to analyze user behavior (Google Ireland Ltd.).
  • Meta Pixel: Used to track ad conversions and for retargeting purposes (Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd.).
  • TikTok Pixel: Used to measure the effectiveness of advertising campaigns (TikTok Technology Ltd.).

sections.heading: 6. Third-Party Communication Services
sections.body:
Cal.com: For appointment scheduling, we process the data you provide for the purpose of contract fulfillment or pre-contractual measures (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR).

Resend: For technical delivery of transactional emails (e.g., form confirmations), we utilize Resend Labs Inc.

Zoom: For video conferencing and online meetings, we use Zoom (Zoom Video Communications, Inc.). This is based on our legitimate interest in efficient communication (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) or contract fulfillment (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR).

sections.heading: 7. Legal Notice (Impressum)
sections.body:
Information according to § 5 TMG:

Jose Francisco Cortez Moreno
Klarastr. 8
12459 Berlin

Person responsible for content according to § 18 MStV:
Jose Francisco Cortez Moreno

EU Dispute Resolution: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (ODR): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/. We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

sections.heading: Person responsible for content according to § 18 Abs. 2 MStV
Dispute Resolution
sections.body:

We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

sections.heading: Liability for Content
sections.body: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with § 7 Para. 1 TMG. According to §§ 8 to 10 TMG, however, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information.
